info.name: CADLens API
info.description:
Auto-generated Postman collection for the CADLens Node API. Maintained by the postman-agent — re-run whenever routes/validators/controllers change.

Quickstart:
1. Set `{{baseUrl}}` (defaults to http://localhost:3001).
2. Complete OAuth via the `01 — OAuth (browser)` folder, then paste the JWT from the `#token=...` redirect fragment into `{{jwt}}`.
3. Hit `POST /v1/keys` to mint an API key — its `key` and `id` are auto-saved to `{{apiKey}}` / `{{keyId}}`.
4. Use `POST /v1/parse` to upload a DWG, DXF, DWF, DGN, DWFx, or PDF file — the returned `job_id` is auto-saved to `{{jobId}}`.

request.description: Liveness probe — returns `{ status: 'ok', db: 'ok' }` when MySQL is reachable.
Public JWKS used by Yii2 / external services to verify Node-issued JWTs.

item.name: 01 — OAuth (browser)
item.description:
OAuth start URLs (`GET /v1/auth/google`, `GET /v1/auth/github`) return a 302 to the provider — open them in a browser. After consent the provider calls the callback URL, which now returns JSON `{ token, user, profileComplete, isNewUser }` directly instead of a redirect fragment. Copy the `token` value and paste it into `{{jwt}}`.

Alternatively, use the `POST .../exchange` endpoints from Postman directly: first obtain a `code` + `state` pair by following the browser flow as far as the provider redirect, then POST them here to receive the token without a browser.

request.description: 302 → Google consent screen. Open in browser; do NOT follow redirects in Postman.

query.description: Authorization code returned by Google. URL-encoded.
Opaque state token originally generated by the server at the start of the flow.

request.description: Google redirects here after consent. Previously this endpoint issued a 302 with a `#token=...` fragment; it now returns JSON directly. Invoke from Postman only if you have a live `code`+`state` pair — each code is single-use and expires in ~60 s.

POST equivalent of the Google callback. Accepts a JSON body with `code` (string, min 1) and `state` (string, min 1) — both validated by `oauthExchangeSchema`. Returns the same JSON shape as the GET callback. Useful for mobile / SPA flows that handle the redirect themselves and need to exchange the code server-side without a browser navigation.
302 → GitHub consent screen. Open in browser; do NOT follow redirects in Postman.

query.description: Opaque state token originally generated by the server at the start of the flow.

request.description: GitHub redirects here after consent. Previously this endpoint issued a 302 with a `#token=...` fragment; it now returns JSON directly. Invoke from Postman only if you have a live `code`+`state` pair — each code is single-use.

POST equivalent of the GitHub callback. Accepts a JSON body with `code` (string, min 1) and `state` (string, min 1) — both validated by `oauthExchangeSchema`. Returns the same JSON shape as the GET callback. Useful for mobile / SPA flows that handle the redirect themselves and need to exchange the code server-side without a browser navigation.
Clears the OAuth nonce cookie. Stateless — JWT remains valid until expiry.
Returns the JWT-resolved user (id, uuid, email, plan, profileComplete).

item.name: 02 — Profile
item.description: Authenticated user profile, usage summary, and invoice history (JWT). Required to satisfy onboarding gating.

request.description: Fetch the current user's profile.

body.raw:
{
  "fullName": "Ada Lovelace",
  "phone": "+1-415-555-0142",
  "country": "US",
  "timezone": "America/Los_Angeles",
  "marketingOptIn": true,
  "companyName": "Analytical Engines, Inc.",
  "companySize": "11-50",
  "industry": "Engineering",
  "vatNumber": "US123456789",
  "billingAddressLine1": "1 Babbage Way",
  "billingAddressLine2": "Suite 1843",
  "billingCity": "San Francisco",
  "billingState": "CA",
  "billingPostalCode": "94105",
  "billingCountry": "US"
}

request.description: Update profile. All fields are optional — see node/src/validators/profile.schema.ts for length and enum constraints.

Returns the current billing-period usage summary for the authenticated user.

Response shape:
```json
{
  "plan": "STARTER",
  "planLabel": "Starter",
  "limit": 500,
  "used": 42,
  "remaining": 458,
  "overageCount": 0,
  "periodStart": "2026-05-01T00:00:00.000Z",
  "periodEnd": "2026-06-01T00:00:00.000Z",
  "renewalDate": "2026-06-01T00:00:00.000Z"
}
```
`limit` and `remaining` are `null` for unlimited plans (e.g. ENTERPRISE). `renewalDate` is the active Stripe subscription's `currentPeriodEnd` when a subscription is present, otherwise the computed period end.

Returns up to 100 billing invoices for the authenticated user, ordered by `periodStart` descending.

Response shape (array):
```json
[
  {
    "id": "inv_1234567890",
    "number": "CAD-0001",
    "status": "paid",
    "amountPaid": 2900,
    "currency": "usd",
    "periodStart": "2026-04-01T00:00:00.000Z",
    "hostedInvoiceUrl": "https://invoice.stripe.com/i/acct_xxx/inv_xxx",
    "invoicePdf": "https://pay.stripe.com/invoice/acct_xxx/inv_xxx/pdf"
  }
]
```
`amountPaid` is in the smallest currency unit (cents for USD). `hostedInvoiceUrl` and `invoicePdf` are `null` when not available (e.g. free-plan period with no charge).

item.name: 03 — API Keys
item.description: Manage API keys (JWT). The plaintext `key` is returned only on creation — store it in `{{apiKey}}` (auto-saved by the test script).

request.description: List the caller's API keys (plaintext key never returned).
Create a new API key. Response includes `id`, `name`, `keyPrefix`, `key` (plaintext, shown once), `createdAt`. The test script saves `key`→`{{apiKey}}` and `id`→`{{keyId}}`.
Revoke an API key. Returns 204 on success.

item.name: 04 — Parse
item.description: Submit a CAD file (DWG, DXF, DWF, DGN, DWFx, or PDF) for parsing. Authenticated with the API key bearer token. Use `mode=async` (default) to return immediately with a `job_id`; use `mode=sync` to long-poll up to 60 s — responds 200 with the full result if the worker finishes in time, or 202 + `job_id` on timeout. Large files auto-divert: uploads of 10 MB or more (and drawings whose converted geometry turns out huge mid-parse) return 202 immediately with a `message` explaining that parsing continues asynchronously — prefer `mode=async` for large files.

formdata.description: Required. Supported formats: DWG, DXF, DWF, DGN, DWFx, PDF. Validated by magic bytes first, then extension.
Optional. Must be a valid URL — receives a POST when the job completes.

Optional. `async` (default) — returns 202 + job_id immediately. `sync` — long-polls up to 60 s; returns 200 with full result body if the worker finishes in time, otherwise 202 + job_id. Uploads of 10 MB or more (or huge converted geometry) skip the wait and return 202 immediately with a `message` — prefer `async` for large files.
Optional — CADLens emails a job link when the parse finishes and the uploader stopped watching.

request.description: Multipart upload. Default `mode=async`: returns 202 `{ job_id, status, fileName, fileSize, createdAt }`. With `mode=sync`: returns 200 with the full result shape (see `GET /v1/jobs/:jobId/result`) if the worker finishes within ~60 s, otherwise 202 `{ job_id, status, fileName, fileSize, createdAt, message }`. The test script saves `job_id`→`{{jobId}}` for both response shapes.

item.name: 05 — Jobs
item.description:
Poll job status and fetch results. All endpoints authenticated with the API key bearer token.

`GET /v1/jobs` and `GET /v1/jobs/:jobId` now both include `imageUrl: string | null` in every job object — a presigned S3 URL (1-hour TTL) for the PNG preview when `status === 'COMPLETED'`, otherwise `null`.

request.description: List the API-key owner's jobs. Returns `{ jobs: [<job>] }` where each job object now includes an extra top-level field `imageUrl: string | null` — a presigned S3 URL (1-hour TTL) when `status === 'COMPLETED'` and a result exists, otherwise `null`.

query.description: Send watch=1 from interactive poll loops — suppresses the notifyEmail when the user watches the job finish live.

request.description: Fetch a single job's status and metadata. The response is the job object directly (not wrapped) and now includes an extra top-level field `imageUrl: string | null` — a presigned S3 URL (1-hour TTL) when `status === 'COMPLETED'` and a result exists, otherwise `null`.

Returns the full parsed result once the job is complete.

Response shape:
```json
{
  "jobId": "job_lH9k2c",
  "status": "COMPLETED",
  "file": { "name": "drawing.dwg", "format": "DWG", "version": "AC1021", "units": "mm" },
  "summary": { "totalSheets": 1, "totalEntities": 142, "totalLayers": 8, "truncated": false },
  "sheets": [
    {
      "name": "Model", "key": "model", "index": 0,
      "imageUrl": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/.../preview-0.png?...",
      "entityCount": 142, "layerCount": 8,
      "boundingBox": { "minX": 0, "minY": 0, "maxX": 500, "maxY": 400, "width": 500, "height": 400 },
      "area": 200000, "perimeter": 1800,
      "layers": [{ "name": "0", "color": 7, "colorHex": "#FFFFFF", "lineType": "CONTINUOUS", "isVisible": true, "entityCount": 142 }],
      "entities": [
        { "type": "LINE", "layer": "0", "layout": "Model", "start": {"x": 0, "y": 0}, "end": {"x": 100, "y": 100} },
        { "type": "HATCH", "layer": "WALL", "layout": "Model", "solid": false, "patternName": "ANSI31",
          "patternAngle": 45, "patternScale": 1,
          "patternLines": [{ "angle": 45, "base": {"x": 0, "y": 0}, "offset": {"x": -2.24, "y": 2.24}, "dashes": [] }],
          "boundaries": [{ "edges": [{ "type": "LWPOLYLINE", "vertices": [{"x":0,"y":0},{"x":50,"y":0},{"x":50,"y":20},{"x":0,"y":20}], "closed": true }] }] }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "metadata": { "filename": "drawing.dwg", "format": "DWG", "units": "mm", "linetypePatterns": { "DASHED": [12.7, -6.35] }, "ltscale": 1, "unsupported3DCount": 0 },
  "imageUrl": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/.../preview-0.png?...",
  "imageUrls": ["https://s3.amazonaws.com/.../preview-0.png?..."]
}
```
`imageUrl` is the first-sheet presigned S3 URL (1-hour TTL, kept for backward compat). `imageUrls` contains one URL per sheet. `entityCount` and `layerCount` are per-sheet fields inside each `sheets[]` entry. Top-level `summary.totalEntities` and `summary.totalLayers` give the drawing-wide totals.

HATCH entities include `patternLines` — the exact pattern-definition line families (angle, base point, offset, dash lengths; drawing units, rotation/scale already applied). `metadata.linetypePatterns` maps linetype names to dash/gap arrays and `metadata.ltscale` is the global $LTSCALE factor. `metadata.unsupported3DCount` counts 3D-only entity types (3DSOLID/BODY/SURFACE/REGION/MESH) with no extractable geometry — such drawings no longer report `metadata.is3D: true`.

response.name: 200 OK — example

originalRequest.description:
Returns the full parsed result once the job is complete.

Response shape:
```json
{
  "jobId": "job_lH9k2c",
  "status": "COMPLETED",
  "file": { "name": "drawing.dwg", "format": "DWG", "version": "AC1021", "units": "mm" },
  "summary": { "totalSheets": 1, "totalEntities": 142, "totalLayers": 8, "truncated": false },
  "sheets": [
    {
      "name": "Model", "key": "model", "index": 0,
      "imageUrl": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/.../preview-0.png?...",
      "entityCount": 142, "layerCount": 8,
      "boundingBox": { "minX": 0, "minY": 0, "maxX": 500, "maxY": 400, "width": 500, "height": 400 },
      "area": 200000, "perimeter": 1800,
      "layers": [{ "name": "0", "color": 7, "colorHex": "#FFFFFF", "lineType": "CONTINUOUS", "isVisible": true, "entityCount": 142 }],
      "entities": [
        { "type": "LINE", "layer": "0", "layout": "Model", "start": {"x": 0, "y": 0}, "end": {"x": 100, "y": 100} },
        { "type": "HATCH", "layer": "WALL", "layout": "Model", "solid": false, "patternName": "ANSI31",
          "patternAngle": 45, "patternScale": 1,
          "patternLines": [{ "angle": 45, "base": {"x": 0, "y": 0}, "offset": {"x": -2.24, "y": 2.24}, "dashes": [] }],
          "boundaries": [{ "edges": [{ "type": "LWPOLYLINE", "vertices": [{"x":0,"y":0},{"x":50,"y":0},{"x":50,"y":20},{"x":0,"y":20}], "closed": true }] }] }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "metadata": { "filename": "drawing.dwg", "format": "DWG", "units": "mm", "linetypePatterns": { "DASHED": [12.7, -6.35] }, "ltscale": 1, "unsupported3DCount": 0 },
  "imageUrl": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/.../preview-0.png?...",
  "imageUrls": ["https://s3.amazonaws.com/.../preview-0.png?..."]
}
```
`imageUrl` is the first-sheet presigned S3 URL (1-hour TTL, kept for backward compat). `imageUrls` contains one URL per sheet. `entityCount` and `layerCount` are per-sheet fields inside each `sheets[]` entry. Top-level `summary.totalEntities` and `summary.totalLayers` give the drawing-wide totals.

HATCH entities include `patternLines` — the exact pattern-definition line families (angle, base point, offset, dash lengths; drawing units, rotation/scale already applied). `metadata.linetypePatterns` maps linetype names to dash/gap arrays and `metadata.ltscale` is the global $LTSCALE factor. `metadata.unsupported3DCount` counts 3D-only entity types (3DSOLID/BODY/SURFACE/REGION/MESH) with no extractable geometry — such drawings no longer report `metadata.is3D: true`.

request.description: Returns presigned S3 URLs for all PNG previews. Response: `{ imageUrl, imageUrls }` where `imageUrl` is the first sheet (backward-compat) and `imageUrls` is an array with one URL per sheet (1-hour TTL).
Delete a job and its S3 artifacts.

item.name: 06 — Billing
item.description:
Stripe-backed billing. The webhook entry is reference-only — Stripe is the real caller and signs requests with `Stripe-Signature`.

**CADLens job webhook** (sent to the customer's `webhookUrl` on job completion):
```json
{
  "eventId": "<uuid>",
  "sequence": 1,
  "event": "job.completed",
  "jobId": "<uuid>",
  "status": "COMPLETED",
  "timestamp": "2026-05-15T12:00:00.000Z",
  "result": {
    "imageUrl": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/.../preview-0.png?...",
    "imageUrls": ["https://s3.amazonaws.com/.../preview-0.png?...", "https://s3.amazonaws.com/.../preview-1.png?..."],
    "file": { "name": "drawing.dwg", "format": "DWG", "version": "AC1021", "units": "mm" },
    "summary": { "totalSheets": 1, "totalEntities": 142, "totalLayers": 8, "truncated": false },
    "sheets": [
      {
        "name": "Model", "index": 0,
        "imageUrl": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/.../preview-0.png?...",
        "entityCount": 142, "layerCount": 8,
        "boundingBox": { "minX": 0, "minY": 0, "maxX": 500, "maxY": 400, "width": 500, "height": 400 },
        "area": 200000, "perimeter": 1800,
        "layers": [{ "name": "0", "color": 7, "colorHex": "#FFFFFF", "lineType": "CONTINUOUS", "isVisible": true, "entityCount": 142 }],
        "entities": [{ "type": "LINE", "layer": "0", "layout": "Model", "start": {"x": 0, "y": 0}, "end": {"x": 100, "y": 100} }]
      }
    ]
  }
}
```
Note: `vectorJson`, `layersJson`, and the root-level `entityCount` / `layerCount` fields have been removed. Entities and layer metadata are now nested inside each `sheets[]` entry. Drawing-wide totals are in `result.summary`. `imageUrl` is the first-sheet presigned S3 URL (backward compat); `imageUrls` contains all sheets.

request.description: Returns a Stripe Customer Portal URL the SPA can redirect to.
Create a Stripe Checkout session for upgrading to a new plan. `plan` is a plan code (case-insensitive, uppercased server-side, e.g. `PRO`). Response: `{ url }` — redirect the browser there.

header.description: Real requests are signed by Stripe — replicating this manually requires HMAC-SHA256 over the raw body using the webhook secret.

request.description:
Reference only — Stripe is the real caller. The route consumes the raw body to verify the `Stripe-Signature` header. Sending from Postman will fail signature verification unless you compute a valid HMAC over the body using your local webhook secret.

For the CADLens → customer job webhook payload shape (sent to the customer's `webhookUrl`), see the `06 — Billing` folder description.

item.name: 07 — Webhooks
item.description: Manage the caller's webhook endpoints (`/v1/me/webhooks`). JWT-authenticated — same bearer token as the dashboard.

request.description: List all webhook endpoints registered for the current user.
Register a new webhook endpoint. `url` must be http(s), max 512 chars. Response is the created endpoint (201).
Update a webhook endpoint. Both fields optional: `isActive` (enable/disable), `setAsDefault` (make this the endpoint used for job notifications).
Delete a webhook endpoint. Returns 204 on success.

item.name: 08 — Activity & Account
item.description: Account-level activity feed and account deletion. JWT-authenticated.

request.description: Unified activity feed: request logs, parse jobs, billing events, and webhook deliveries for the current user.
Permanently delete the account: cancels any active Stripe subscription and purges all associated data. Returns 204 on success. Irreversible.

item.name: 09 — Public Marketing
item.description: Public, unauthenticated endpoints that drive the marketing site (pricing table, beta waitlist, contact form).

request.description: Public list of active plans — drives the marketing pricing page and dashboard plan list.
Join the beta waitlist. `use_case` is optional free text (max 100 chars).
Submit the public contact form. `turnstile_token` is a Cloudflare Turnstile CAPTCHA token (required in production; skipped in dev when TURNSTILE_SECRET_KEY is unset).
